ogTitle: Ogunbil vs Old Adaminaby: Climate Safety

heading: Ogunbil vs Old Adaminaby — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ogunbil, New South Wales and Old Adaminaby, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ogunbil has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Old Adaminaby (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ogunbil or Old Adaminaby safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ogunbil has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Old Adaminaby scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ogunbil scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Old Adaminaby scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ogunbil or Old Adaminaby?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ogunbil has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.27% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
